title: Make manifest and filesystem reconciliation explicit
description:
Created from the project state audit.

Fix workflows where filesystem changes exist but do not appear in the manifest-backed Pinake tree.

Context:
- `newFolder` creates empty folders but the TreeView uses manifest children whenever manifest documents exist, so empty folders can be hidden.
- Directory duplicate copies files and rebuilds the index, but copied Markdown files are not added to `pinake.json`, so duplicated folders can disappear from the TreeView.
- Repair/import should provide a deliberate path for untracked Markdown files instead of relying on incidental filesystem fallback behavior.

Implementation notes:
- Merge filesystem directories into manifest-backed tree children, including empty folders.
- When duplicating directories, add copied Markdown documents to `pinake.json` with stable generated metadata.
- Keep index rebuild behavior after bulk directory operations.
- Decide whether repair should discover untracked Markdown files or whether this belongs to import.
- Add tests for new empty folder visibility, directory duplication visibility, and untracked Markdown handling.

Code anchors: `src/tree/PinakeTreeProvider.ts:133`, `src/commands/PinakesCommands.ts:170`, `src/commands/PinakesCommands.ts:390`.

checklist.text: Show filesystem directories alongside manifest-backed children
Add duplicated directory Markdown files to `pinake.json`
Define repair/import behavior for untracked Markdown files
Add tests for empty folders, directory duplicate, and untracked document visibility
